questions.question: If you delete your account, what happens to private workspaces where you were the only member?

options.text: They are archived and remain accessible to you.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Private workspaces where you are the only member are permanently deleted.

options.text: They are shared with all your contacts automatically.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are deleted, not shared.

options.text: They are deleted.
options.explanation: Correct. According to the article, any private workspaces in which you were the only member will be deleted.

options.text: They are transferred to your email address.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are deleted, not transferred.

questions.question: How long does Notion keep backups to restore deleted content?

options.text: 7 days
options.explanation: Incorrect. The backup window is 30 days, not 7.

options.text: 30 days
options.explanation: Correct. Notion keeps backups that allow restoration of content from the past 30 days.

options.text: 60 days
options.explanation: Incorrect. The restoration window is 30 days, not 60.

options.text: Indefinitely
options.explanation: Incorrect. Backups are only available for the past 30 days.

questions.question: When you move a top-level page to another workspace, how are its sub-pages handled?

options.text: They are left behind in the original workspace.
options.explanation: Incorrect. All sub-pages move with the top-level page.

options.text: They are deleted.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The sub-pages are moved, not deleted.

options.text: They move with the top-level page.
options.explanation: Correct. The article states that when you move top-level pages, all their sub-pages go with them.

options.text: They are duplicated in the new workspace.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are moved, not duplicated.

questions.question: Can you access private pages after deleting your account?

options.text: Yes, if you exported them before deleting.
options.explanation: Incorrect. You cannot access content from workspaces that are deleted or from which you are removed, even private pages.

options.text: No, even private pages are gone forever.
options.explanation: Correct. The article explicitly states you won't be able to access any content from workspaces that are deleted or that you are removed from — even your private pages.

options.text: Yes, as long as you were a member of a shared workspace.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Your access is revoked regardless of whether the pages were in private or shared spaces.

options.text: Yes, until the 30-day backup window expires.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Access is lost immediately upon deletion; the 30-day window applies to restoration requests, not active access.

questions.question: What happens to a shared workspace if you were the sole admin and deleted your account?

options.text: It remains active for other members.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Since the admin (you) is deleted, the workspace is deleted for everyone.

options.text: It is deleted for everyone.
options.explanation: Correct. Any shared workspaces in which you were the only admin will be deleted, and deleted workspaces are deleted for everyone in them.

options.text: It is archived for 30 days.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Workspaces are permanently deleted, not archived.

options.text: It is transferred to the next admin in the list.
options.explanation: Incorrect. There is no mechanism to transfer ownership upon account deletion.

questions.question: Where is the 'Delete my account' option located in the Notion sidebar?

options.text: Top right corner of the screen
options.explanation: Incorrect. You must go to Settings in the sidebar, then click on your name.

options.text: Settings in your sidebar → {your name}
options.explanation: Correct. The steps are: Settings in your sidebar → {your name} → Delete my account.

options.text: Help menu at the bottom of the sidebar
options.explanation: Incorrect. The Help menu is used for contacting support, not for account deletion.

options.text: Account settings page
options.explanation: Incorrect. It is accessed via the sidebar menu under your name, not a dedicated settings page.

questions.question: When migrating content to a different workspace, what is a potential issue mentioned?

options.text: The content is permanently lost during the transfer.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The content is duplicated to the new workspace, though it may have issues.

options.text: The content is duplicated and sometimes has issues.
options.explanation: Correct. The article notes that any migrated content will be duplicated and these pages sometimes have issues.

options.text: The content is converted to image files automatically.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The content is duplicated, not converted to images.

options.text: The content is shared with your contacts automatically.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The content is moved to a new workspace, not shared with contacts.

questions.question: Which of the following is a mandatory step to confirm the account deletion?

options.text: Enter your password
options.explanation: Incorrect. You must manually enter your account email, not your password.

options.text: Select a reason for deletion
options.explanation: Incorrect. No reason selection is required; you must enter your email.

options.text: Manually enter your account email
options.explanation: Correct. A window pops up to confirm workspaces, and you must manually enter your account email to proceed.

options.text: Verify via SMS
options.explanation: Incorrect. There is no SMS verification step mentioned.

questions.question: What does Notion show you in the warning window before you delete your account?

options.text: A list of all your passwords
options.explanation: Incorrect. The warning lists which workspaces will be deleted, not passwords.

options.text: A list of which workspaces will be deleted
options.explanation: Correct. You will see a warning about which of your workspaces will be deleted.

options.text: A warning about internet speed
options.explanation: Incorrect. There is no warning about internet speed.

options.text: A request for your phone number
options.explanation: Incorrect. No phone number is requested.

questions.question: After moving top-level pages to another workspace, where do they initially appear?

options.text: The main dashboard
options.explanation: Incorrect. They do not appear on the main dashboard by default.

options.text: The `Private` section
options.explanation: Correct. They appear in the `Private` section but can be moved wherever you want.

options.text: The `Deleted` folder
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are not in a deleted folder; they are moved to the new workspace.

options.text: The `Shared` section
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are private to your new account until you move them.

questions.question: How can you contact Notion support to request restoration of accidentally deleted content?

options.text: Only via email
options.explanation: Incorrect. You can use either the app help menu or email.

options.text: Only by phone call
options.explanation: Incorrect. You must use the app or email.

options.text: Both the app help menu and email
options.explanation: Correct. You can send a message in the app by clicking `?` at the bottom of the sidebar or email us.

options.text: Only through a contact form on the website
options.explanation: Incorrect. The app menu or email are the valid methods.
